# I/O Utility Operators Parity Contracts

**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.2.3` ([T2.3])
**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
**Date**: 2026-03-03
**Dependencies**: T2.2 (core AsyncRead/AsyncWrite trait-semantic gaps)
**Purpose**: Define executable parity contracts for copy/split/lines/buf/stream-adapter utility operators under backpressure and cancellation. Validates no hidden obligation leaks and no orphaned internal tasks.

---

## 1. Scope

This document covers I/O utility operators that combine, split, or adapt async streams:

| Category | Operators | Module | LOC |
|----------|----------|--------|-----|
| Copy | `copy`, `copy_buf`, `copy_bidirectional`, `copy_with_progress` | `io/copy.rs` | ~450 |
| Split | `SplitStream`, `ReadHalf`, `WriteHalf` | `io/split.rs` | ~180 |
| Lines | `Lines<R>` iterator | `io/lines.rs` | ~85 |
| Buffering | `BufReader`, `BufWriter` | `io/buf_reader.rs`, `io/buf_writer.rs` | ~400 |
| Stream Adapters | `ReaderStream`, `StreamReader` | `io/stream_adapters.rs` | ~200 |
| Write Permit | `WritePermit` (asupersync-specific) | `io/write_permit.rs` | ~150 |
| **Total** | | | **~1,465** |

### 1.1 Out of Scope

- Core traits (`AsyncRead`, `AsyncWrite`, `AsyncSeek`, `AsyncBufRead`) — covered by T2.2
- Extension method parity (`read_exact`, `write_all`, integer I/O) — covered by T2.2
- Codec/framing (`Framed`, `LengthDelimited`, `LinesCodec`) — covered by T2.4
- Capability model (`IoCap`, `LabIoCap`) — architectural, not parity

---

## 2. Copy Operator Contracts

### 2.1 `copy(reader, writer)` → `io::Result<u64>`

| Property | Contract | Tokio Equivalent |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| Return value | Total bytes transferred | Same |
| Cancel safety | **Cancel-safe**: committed bytes remain in writer | Same |
| Backpressure | Yields after 32 iterations to prevent starvation | Tokio uses internal yield counter |
| Buffer size | 8192 bytes | Same |
| EOF handling | Returns Ok(0) when reader reaches EOF | Same |
| Error propagation | First error terminates; committed bytes are not rolled back | Same |

### 2.2 `copy_buf(reader, writer)` → `io::Result<u64>`

| Property | Contract | Tokio Equivalent |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| Requirement | Reader must implement `AsyncBufRead` | Same |
| Efficiency | Zero-copy from reader's internal buffer | Same |
| Cancel safety | **Cancel-safe**: same as `copy` | Same |
| Flush | Flushes writer before returning | Same |

### 2.3 `copy_bidirectional(a, b)` → `io::Result<(u64, u64)>`

| Property | Contract | Tokio Equivalent |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| Return value | Tuple of (a→b bytes, b→a bytes) | Same |
| Cancel safety | **Cancel-safe**: each direction independently committed | Same |
| Completion | Returns when either direction reaches EOF or error | Same |
| Independence | Both directions proceed concurrently | Same |

### 2.4 `copy_with_progress(reader, writer, callback)` → `io::Result<u64>`

| Property | Contract | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|
| Callback | Invoked with cumulative byte count after each write | Asupersync-specific extension |
| Cancel safety | **Cancel-safe**: callback count matches committed bytes | N/A in tokio |

---

## 3. Split Operator Contracts

### 3.1 Current Implementation (`SplitStream` / borrowing split)

| Property | Contract | Tokio Equivalent |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| Mechanism | `RefCell`-based interior mutability | Tokio uses `Arc<Mutex>` for `into_split` |
| Thread safety | **Single-thread only**; panics on concurrent borrow | Tokio's `into_split` is `Send + Sync` |
| Lifetime | Halves borrow from `SplitStream` wrapper | Tokio's owned halves are `'static` |
| Reassembly | `into_inner()` on wrapper | `reunite()` on halves |

### 3.2 Gap: IO-G9 (`into_split`)

| Property | Required Contract |
|----------|-----------------|
| Ownership | Owned halves: `OwnedReadHalf<T>` and `OwnedWriteHalf<T>` |
| Thread safety | Must be `Send + Sync` (requires `Arc<Mutex<T>>`) |
| Cancel safety | Halves must tolerate being dropped independently |
| Reunite | `reunite(read, write)` → `Result<T, ReuniteError>` |
| Priority | **HIGH** — blocks bidirectional protocol patterns (WebSocket, gRPC, DB replication) |

### 3.3 Invariants

| ID | Invariant | Check |
|----|-----------|-------|
| SPLIT-1 | ReadHalf produces same bytes as underlying reader | Compare with direct read |
| SPLIT-2 | WriteHalf commits same bytes as underlying writer | Compare with direct write |
| SPLIT-3 | `into_inner()` returns stream with preserved state | Verify read_pos, written data |
| SPLIT-4 | Dropping one half does not corrupt the other | Use remaining half after drop |

---

## 4. Lines Iterator Contracts

### 4.1 `Lines<R>` where `R: AsyncBufRead`

| Property | Contract | Tokio Equivalent |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| Item type | `Stream<Item = io::Result<String>>` | Same |
| Line endings | Handles `\n` and `\r\n` (strips both) | Same |
| Final line | Returns last line even without trailing newline | Same |
| Empty input | Returns `None` immediately | Same |
| UTF-8 errors | Returns `io::ErrorKind::InvalidData` | Same |
| Cancel safety | **Cancel-safe**: buffered bytes preserved across polls | Same |

### 4.2 Gap: IO-G7 (AsyncBufReadExt convenience methods)

| Method | Description | Priority |
|--------|------------|----------|
| `read_until(byte, &mut buf)` | Read until delimiter byte | Medium |
| `read_line(&mut buf)` | Read one line into String | Medium |
| `split(byte)` | Iterator split on arbitrary byte | Medium |

### 4.3 Invariants

| ID | Invariant | Check |
|----|-----------|-------|
| LINES-1 | `\r\n` stripped to empty, not `\r` | Verify CRLF handling |
| LINES-2 | EOF without newline emits final line | Verify incomplete last line |
| LINES-3 | Empty reader yields None (no empty string) | Verify empty input |
| LINES-4 | Invalid UTF-8 produces error, not panic | Verify error variant |

---

## 5. Buffered I/O Contracts

### 5.1 `BufReader<R>`

| Property | Contract | Tokio Equivalent |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| Default capacity | 8192 bytes | Same |
| `AsyncBufRead` impl | `poll_fill_buf` returns internal buffer ref | Same |
| Cancel safety | **Cancel-safe**: buffer state preserved | Same |
| EOF | Returns empty slice | Same |
| `get_ref()` / `get_mut()` | Access underlying reader | Same |

### 5.2 `BufWriter<W>`

| Property | Contract | Tokio Equivalent |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| Default capacity | 8192 bytes | Same |
| Auto-flush | Writes to underlying writer when buffer full | Same |
| Cancel safety | `poll_flush` is cancel-safe | Same |
| Data loss on drop | Buffered data lost if not flushed | Same (documented) |
| `get_ref()` / `get_mut()` | Access underlying writer | Same |

### 5.3 Invariants

| ID | Invariant | Check |
|----|-----------|-------|
| BUF-1 | BufReader: filled buffer matches underlying read | Compare |
| BUF-2 | BufWriter: flushed bytes match cumulative writes | Compare |
| BUF-3 | BufWriter: drop without flush loses buffered data | Verify |
| BUF-4 | BufReader: multiple fills accumulate correctly | Sequential reads |

---

## 6. Stream Adapter Contracts

### 6.1 `ReaderStream<R>` (AsyncRead → Stream)

| Property | Contract | Tokio Equivalent |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| Chunk size | Default 8192, configurable via `with_capacity` | Same |
| Item type | `Stream<Item = io::Result<Vec<u8>>>` | Tokio uses `Bytes` |
| EOF | Returns `None` | Same |
| Error | Returns error item then `None` | Same |
| Cancel safety | **Cancel-safe**: partial read discarded on cancel | Same |

### 6.2 `StreamReader<S>` (Stream → AsyncRead)

| Property | Contract | Tokio Equivalent |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| Buffering | Holds current chunk; consumes from offset | Same |
| Pending | Returns `Pending` if no buffered data and stream pending | Same |
| Partial delivery | Returns already-buffered data before surfacing errors | Same |
| Error deferral | If data buffered, returns data first; error on next read | Same |
| Cancel safety | **Cancel-safe**: buffered offset preserved | Same |

### 6.3 Invariants

| ID | Invariant | Check |
|----|-----------|-------|
| ADAPT-1 | `ReaderStream` round-trip: concatenated chunks == original data | Verify |
| ADAPT-2 | `StreamReader` round-trip: read output == concatenated stream chunks | Verify |
| ADAPT-3 | `StreamReader` defers error until buffered data consumed | Verify error sequencing |
| ADAPT-4 | `ReaderStream` handles zero-byte reads correctly (no infinite loop) | Verify termination |

---

## 7. Remaining Gaps Summary

| ID | Description | Severity | Effort | Blocks |
|----|------------|----------|--------|--------|
| IO-G9 | `into_split()` — Arc-based owned split halves | HIGH | 3-5 days | WebSocket, gRPC bidirectional |
| IO-G11 | `DuplexStream` / `SimplexStream` | HIGH | 2-3 days | Integration test infrastructure |
| IO-G7 | `read_until`, `read_line`, `split(byte)` | MEDIUM | 1-2 days | Convenience for protocol parsing |
| IO-G8 | `BufStream<RW>` combinator | LOW | <1 day | Combined buffering |
| IO-G10 | `Empty`, `Repeat`, `Sink` trivial adapters | LOW | <1 day | Test fixtures |

---

## 8. Cancel-Safety Matrix

| Operator | Cancel-Safe? | Behavior on Cancel |
|----------|-------------|-------------------|
| `copy` | Yes | Committed bytes remain |
| `copy_buf` | Yes | Committed bytes remain |
| `copy_bidirectional` | Yes | Each direction independent |
| `SplitStream::split()` | Yes | Structural only |
| `Lines::poll_next` | Yes | Buffer preserved |
| `BufReader::poll_fill_buf` | Yes | Buffer preserved |
| `BufWriter::poll_flush` | Yes | Can retry |
| `ReaderStream::poll_next` | Yes | Partial read discarded |
| `StreamReader::poll_read` | Yes | Offset preserved |
| `WritePermit::commit` | Yes | Uncommitted data discarded |

---

## 9. Obligation and Task Leak Analysis

| Operator | Internal Tasks | Leak Risk |
|----------|---------------|-----------|
| `copy` / `copy_buf` | None (synchronous poll loop) | None |
| `copy_bidirectional` | None (poll loop with two directions) | None |
| `SplitStream` | None (RefCell wrapper) | None |
| `Lines` | None (poll loop with buffer) | None |
| `BufReader` / `BufWriter` | None (synchronous buffering) | None |
| `ReaderStream` | None (poll adapter) | None |
| `StreamReader` | None (poll adapter with buffer) | None |
| `WritePermit` | None (two-phase commit state) | None |

All utility operators are **obligation-leak-free**: none spawn internal tasks, none hold runtime resources beyond the underlying stream, and all are purely poll-driven.

---

## 10. Drift Detection

| Rule | Trigger | Action |
|------|---------|--------|
| DRIFT-U1 | New utility operator added to `io/` | Add contract row and invariant test |
| DRIFT-U2 | Cancel-safety annotation changed | Update cancel-safety matrix |
| DRIFT-U3 | `into_split` (IO-G9) implemented | Add owned-split invariant tests |
| DRIFT-U4 | `DuplexStream` (IO-G11) implemented | Add round-trip tests |
| DRIFT-U5 | Buffer default changed | Update BUF-* invariant thresholds |
